description: Sector-specific screening controls for UK insurers, insurance intermediaries, policyholder documents, underwriting, claims, market conduct, prudential controls, and data protection.

metadata.scope: UK sector overlay controls for Insurance Sector Compliance Overlay. Intended to be used with uk_core_control_library.
metadata.source_note: Source basis is mapped from official UK legal/regulatory sources available at rule-pack publication time. This starter pack is not legal advice and requires expert validation before production reliance.
metadata.external_knowledge_policy: No external knowledge is introduced during document evaluation. Regulatory source mappings live only in this versioned rule pack.
metadata.territorial_scope_note: UK law can differ across England, Wales, Scotland, and Northern Ireland. This starter pack uses UK-wide sources where available and flags devolved or nation-specific application for human review.

primary_sources.name: PRA prudential regulation
primary_sources.issuer: Prudential Regulation Authority / Bank of England

rules.title: FCA/PRA authorisation or insurance permission evidence is present
rules.summary: Checks for FCA/PRA authorisation, insurance permissions, insurer/intermediary status, or regulatory reference evidence.

metadata.evidence_expectation: The reviewed document/document set should contain direct textual evidence for this control, or the engine should return missing/review_required according to the rule strategy.
metadata.review_guidance: A qualified UK compliance/legal reviewer must confirm applicability, legal sufficiency, territorial scope, and whether absence of textual evidence should be treated as non-compliance.

rules.title: ICOBS customer disclosure and suitability evidence is present
rules.summary: Checks for ICOBS, appropriate information, suitability, policy summary, exclusions, price disclosure, or customer decision evidence.

metadata.evidence_expectation: The reviewed document/document set should contain direct textual evidence for this control, or the engine should return missing/review_required according to the rule strategy.
metadata.review_guidance: A qualified UK compliance/legal reviewer must confirm applicability, legal sufficiency, territorial scope, and whether absence of textual evidence should be treated as non-compliance.
source_basis: FCA Insurance Conduct of Business Sourcebook

rules.title: Claims handling and fair-treatment evidence is present
rules.summary: Checks for claims handling, fair treatment, complaint, settlement, repudiation, or redress evidence.

metadata.evidence_expectation: The reviewed document/document set should contain direct textual evidence for this control, or the engine should return missing/review_required according to the rule strategy.
metadata.review_guidance: A qualified UK compliance/legal reviewer must confirm applicability, legal sufficiency, territorial scope, and whether absence of textual evidence should be treated as non-compliance.

rules.title: Solvency/prudential governance evidence is present where applicable
rules.summary: Checks for Solvency II, capital requirement, ORSA, actuarial function, risk management, or PRA reporting evidence.

metadata.evidence_expectation: The reviewed document/document set should contain direct textual evidence for this control, or the engine should return missing/review_required according to the rule strategy.
metadata.review_guidance: A qualified UK compliance/legal reviewer must confirm applicability, legal sufficiency, territorial scope, and whether absence of textual evidence should be treated as non-compliance.

rules.title: Policyholder and claims-data privacy evidence is present
rules.summary: Checks for policyholder privacy, claims data handling, special category data, data sharing, or processor controls.

metadata.evidence_expectation: The reviewed document/document set should contain direct textual evidence for this control, or the engine should return missing/review_required according to the rule strategy.
metadata.review_guidance: A qualified UK compliance/legal reviewer must confirm applicability, legal sufficiency, territorial scope, and whether absence of textual evidence should be treated as non-compliance.
